name: Mountain Holly
summary: The upland deciduous holly named in PA DCNR Northern Hardwood Forest community descriptions — not to be confused with Ilex mucronata (a wetland obligate species in a different community). Mid-layer shrub or small tree, 10–20 ft.
notes: Inconspicuous spring flowers; bright orange-red berries in fall consumed by thrushes, grouse, and small mammals. Smooth gray bark; serrated elliptical leaves. Dioecious — plant at least one male per several females for fruiting. A canonical reference-community species long absent from most restoration plantings.

planting.site_notes: Dioecious — buy named male and female stock for reliable fruiting. Often confused with Ilex mucronata (Nemopanthus) which is a wetland obligate of sphagnum bog communities, NOT this upland species. Wire cage; some deer browse on new growth.
